payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new for Sam from Goldman because it is the newer/current memory. mem_old was filtered out because it is superseded and should not be surfaced as current.
citations: Input: 'Explain why recall returned mem_new for Sam from Goldman and filtered mem_old because it is superseded'
payload.decision: Recall preferred mem_new because it is the newer, current memory for Sam from Goldman; mem_old was excluded because it has been superseded and should not be treated as current.

entity_resolution.reason: The two memories appear to describe the same underlying subject, Sam from Goldman, but mem_old is superseded, so recall should keep mem_new as the active version.

payload.intent: Explain a recall result where mem_new for Sam from Goldman was returned and mem_old was filtered out as superseded.

memory_cards.statement: mem_new is the current memory for Sam from Goldman and was returned by recall.
mem_old is an older memory about Sam from Goldman and was filtered because it is superseded.

details: Identified mem_new as the returned current memory.
Identified mem_old as filtered due to supersession.
Preserved the distinction between current and superseded facts.

repair_options: Keep mem_new as the canonical current card for Sam from Goldman.
If both cards must remain, link mem_old to mem_new with a supersedes relationship.
